mission.title: Install and Configure Ingress NGINX Controller
mission.description: Ingress NGINX Controller is a Kubernetes Ingress controller that uses NGINX as a reverse proxy and load balancer. It is suitable for managing external access to services in a Kubernetes cluster. However, it is important to note that this project is retiring, and users are encouraged to transition to a Gateway API implementation.

steps.title: Add the Ingress NGINX Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Ingress NGINX Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add ingress-nginx https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/main/deploy/helm-chart/ingress-nginx

```

> This command adds the repository for the Ingress NGINX Helm chart.

steps.title: Install the Ingress NGINX Controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Ingress NGINX Controller using Helm in the 'ingress-nginx' namespace.

```bash

helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx --create-namespace --version 4.10.1

```

> This command installs the Ingress NGINX Controller with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Ingress NGINX Controller pods are running successfully.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> You should see the Ingress NGINX Controller pods in a Running state.

steps.title: Create an example Ingress resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Ingress resource to test the Ingress NGINX Controller.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

```

> This command creates an Ingress resource that routes traffic to 'example-service'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Ingress NGINX Controller
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ingress NGINX Controller and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete any Ingress resources created.

```bash

helm uninstall ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx

kubectl delete namespace ingress-nginx

```

> This command uninstalls the Ingress NGINX Controller.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Ingress NGINX Controller
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing configurations, upgrading the controller, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get ingress -A -o yaml > ingress-backup.yaml

helm upgrade ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx --version 4.11.0

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> Backup existing Ingress resources.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the Ingress NGINX Controller pods are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Ingress" in version "networking.k8s.io/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the Kubernetes version is incompatible with the Ingress resource version.

**Fix:**
Ensure your Kubernetes version is 1.19 or higher and that the Ingress resource is defined correctly.

**Affected versions:** 1.25+

troubleshooting.title: Ingress resource not routing traffic as expected.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the service referenced in the Ingress resource is not available.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'kubectl get svc' and ensure the service is running.

**Affected versions:** 1.25+

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ingress NGINX Controller is unable to communicate with the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Check network policies and ensure that the Ingress NGINX Controller has the necessary permissions.

**Affected versions:** 1.25+

troubleshooting.title: Pod crash looping with exit code 1.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to misconfiguration in the Ingress resource or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Review the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ingress-nginx' for more details.

**Affected versions:** 1.25+

versionNotes.changes: Updated NGINX to 1.23.1, improved health checks, added support for custom error pages.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required unless downgrading from a higher version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Ingress NGINX Controller pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and Helm installed.
